title: Recursion should not be infinite
description: Recursion should not be infiniteAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?Recursion happens when control enters a loop that has no exit. It can occur when a method invokes itself, when two methods invoke each other, or whengotostatements are used to move between two code segments. Recursion can be a useful tool, but unless the method includes a provision to break out the recursion andreturn, the recursion will continue until the stack overflows and the program crashes.Noncompliant code exampleint pow(int num, int exponent) { // Noncompliant: pow is always re-called num = num * pow(num, exponent-1); return num; // this is never reached } void internalRecursion(int i) { start: goto end; end: goto start; // Noncompliant: there is no way to break out o
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Function contains infinite recursion - missing or unreachable base case
Infinite goto loop detected - no exit condition provided
Mutual infinite recursion detected between functions
Missing or unreachable base case leads to infinite recursion
Recursive function lacks proper termination condition
